human_required.expected_evidence: Dated APPROVE/REJECT record with approved scope, exceptions, rollback owner, and dependency decision.
human_required.release_impact: Blocks only the corresponding architecture, production, live-provider, migration, or UX claim until evidence exists.
human_required.why_ai_cannot_verify: JiYong/Tars or authorized owner approves the architecture, dependency, migration scope, rollback plan, and security ownership before code changes that cross the gate.

human_required.expected_evidence: Signed report, test scope/topology, findings, remediation, re-test evidence.
human_required.release_impact: Blocks only the corresponding architecture, production, live-provider, migration, or UX claim until evidence exists.
human_required.why_ai_cannot_verify: No cross-tenant data/metadata leak or policy bypass is found, or findings are remediated and re-tested.

human_required.expected_evidence: Redacted login/mapping/revocation transcript and owner/security approval.
human_required.release_impact: Blocks only the corresponding architecture, production, live-provider, migration, or UX claim until evidence exists.
human_required.why_ai_cannot_verify: Authorized stakeholders confirm mappings and revocation behavior; no synthetic-fixture claim is promoted to real IdP readiness.

human_required.expected_evidence: Topology diagram, firewall/proxy/network-policy evidence, packet/log transcript, approval.
human_required.release_impact: Blocks only the corresponding architecture, production, live-provider, migration, or UX claim until evidence exists.
human_required.required_human_action: Network control review and egress test.
human_required.why_ai_cannot_verify: All undeclared paths are blocked and approved paths are traceable under the real topology.

human_required.expected_evidence: Redacted provider transcript, approval, ConnectorHub/CornerStone audit refs, result/evidence refs.
human_required.release_impact: Blocks only the corresponding architecture, production, live-provider, migration, or UX claim until evidence exists.
human_required.why_ai_cannot_verify: Credential custody, declared capability, source policy, product approval, egress, result, evidence, and audit boundaries hold without secret exposure.

human_required.expected_evidence: ACCEPT/REJECT note, screenshots/recording, task outcomes, confusion/issues list.
human_required.release_impact: Blocks only the corresponding architecture, production, live-provider, migration, or UX claim until evidence exists.
human_required.why_ai_cannot_verify: They accept or reject whether boundaries, causes, risks, and recovery paths are understandable and not misleading.

human_required.expected_evidence: Signed transcript, before/after counts/hashes, RLS/policy inventory, restore/rollback result.
human_required.release_impact: Blocks only the corresponding architecture, production, live-provider, migration, or UX claim until evidence exists.
human_required.why_ai_cannot_verify: Data/evidence/audit integrity, tenant ownership, RLS policies, and service recovery meet the approved plan.

required_before_pass: H02-H07 evidence before claiming production security, real IdP, production network, live provider, human UX, or migration readiness.

scenario_results.given: The same authenticated principal, tenant, namespace, resource, and action are available through CLI, API, and UI.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Shared RequestContext, policy client, CLI adapter, API middleware, UI error mapping.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Three transcripts, normalized request-context digest, matching decision outcome/revision, exit/status codes, audit references.
scenario_results.then: All surfaces derive the same trusted request context, receive the same policy outcome and stable error semantics, and emit linked policy/audit references.
scenario_results.verification_method: Automated CLI/API/browser integration test using one fixture principal and normalized input comparison.
scenario_results.when: The operation is submitted through each surface.

scenario_results.given: A caller is authenticated for tenant A and supplies tenant B, owner, role, or classification values in a header, body, query, or CLI flag.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Identity-to-scope resolver, input allowlist, anti-confused-deputy validation.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Denied response, zero tenant-B rows/objects, policy decision, audit event, negative-evidence counters.
scenario_results.then: Authoritative scope comes only from trusted identity/membership state; forged attributes are ignored or rejected, tenant B is not accessed, and the attempt is audited.
scenario_results.verification_method: Parameterized API/CLI tests for every client-controlled scope field and direct service invocation.
scenario_results.when: The caller invokes a protected read, write, policy, tool, or action operation.

scenario_results.given: A protected request has missing, malformed, expired, conflicting, or unresolvable principal/tenant context.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Request validation, principal resolver, policy fallback, worker/tool guards.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Stable error codes, zero DB/egress calls, sanitized policy/audit records.
scenario_results.then: The system fails closed before protected database access or egress, returns a stable helpful error, and records no secret-bearing input.
scenario_results.verification_method: Unit and integration matrix for null, empty, malformed, expired, and conflicting context.
scenario_results.when: The request reaches the gateway, service, worker, or tool entry point.

scenario_results.given: A truth-bearing object is created or migrated: Artifact, DerivedRepresentation, SearchSnapshot, EvidenceBundle, Claim, ontology object/link, ActionCard, WorkflowRun, PolicyDecision, or AuditEvent.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Domain models, database constraints, serializer validation, migration checks.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Schema inventory, constraint output, representative rows/API payloads, failed-null inserts.
scenario_results.then: Required tenant_id, namespace_id, owner_id, and applicable workspace/classification fields are non-null, validated, immutable where required, and visible in API/CLI evidence.
scenario_results.verification_method: Schema tests plus create/read tests for every active durable object type.
scenario_results.when: The object is persisted.

scenario_results.given: A principal's membership, role, attribute, capability, or workspace access is revoked.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Membership store, token/session versioning, policy cache invalidation, worker lease checks.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Before/after decisions, cache revision/expiry, denial audit, zero post-revocation access.
scenario_results.then: The new request is denied within the documented revocation window; stale authorization state cannot preserve access.
scenario_results.verification_method: Integration test with allow → revoke → retry across API, CLI, service, and worker paths.
scenario_results.when: The principal repeats a previously allowed request, including through a cached session or long-running worker.

scenario_results.given: Any protected operation is evaluated.
scenario_results.implementation_area: PolicyDecision schema, OPA result adapter, persistence, redaction.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: PolicyDecision JSON fixtures, OPA decision_id/revision, audit linkage, redaction proof.
scenario_results.then: A canonical PolicyDecision records allow/deny/escalate, reason codes, safe resolution guidance, policy path, bundle revision/hash, decision_id, input digest, timestamps, tenant/namespace, and evidence/audit refs.
scenario_results.verification_method: Schema contract tests and golden JSON tests for allow, deny, escalate, undefined, and error results.
scenario_results.when: OPA and the CornerStone policy adapter return a result.

scenario_results.given: Tenant A and tenant B each have rows in every active tenant-bearing table.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Postgres RLS policies, transaction-local tenant context, repository adapters.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: SQL transcript, row counts, pg_policies inventory, zero tenant-B identifiers.
scenario_results.then: Only tenant-A rows are visible; tenant-B rows and existence metadata are absent.
scenario_results.verification_method: Database integration matrix across every RLS table using the real application role.
scenario_results.when: The application database role executes SELECT as tenant A.

scenario_results.given: The application role is scoped to tenant A.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Command-specific RLS policies, immutable tenant key constraints, transaction handling.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: SQLSTATE/neutral API error, unchanged before/after snapshots, rollback evidence, audit event.
scenario_results.then: INSERT/UPDATE are rejected by WITH CHECK or equivalent; cross-tenant DELETE affects zero rows or is denied; no partial mutation occurs.
scenario_results.verification_method: Database tests for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, including bulk statements and RETURNING.
scenario_results.when: It attempts INSERT with tenant B, UPDATE a row into tenant B, or DELETE tenant-B data.

scenario_results.given: Tenant A queries tables containing tenant-B data through joins, subqueries, aggregates, counts, groupings, existence checks, and pagination.
scenario_results.implementation_area: RLS-safe queries, tenant-scoped pagination/cursors, neutral errors.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Result snapshots, count/cursor assertions, no foreign IDs, sanitized errors.
scenario_results.then: Results, counts, cursors, totals, and timing/error text do not reveal tenant-B data or existence.
scenario_results.verification_method: Parameterized SQL/API integration tests including empty-result and boundary-page cases.
scenario_results.when: The query is executed through supported repository and API paths.

scenario_results.given: FTS, vector/semantic search, ontology search, autocomplete, and saved search snapshots contain content from two tenants.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Tenant-bearing search tables/indexes, RLS-safe search queries, tenant-scoped embeddings/cache.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Search/API transcripts, query plans or SQL trace, zero canary leakage, snapshot scope.
scenario_results.then: No tenant-B result, snippet, score, suggestion, facet count, object, or snapshot reference is returned.
scenario_results.verification_method: End-to-end search tests with unique canary terms in each tenant.
scenario_results.when: Tenant A searches using terms unique to tenant B.

scenario_results.given: Tenant B owns an original blob, derived representation, EvidenceBundle item, or storage URI referenced by an identifier guessed by tenant A.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Artifact service policy, tenant-scoped storage keys/signing, evidence traversal checks.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Denied responses, zero bytes returned, storage access log, policy/audit refs.
scenario_results.then: Access is denied without revealing content or sensitive metadata; storage access is bound to the authorized tenant and object.
scenario_results.verification_method: API/object-store adapter tests using guessed IDs and cross-tenant evidence links.
scenario_results.when: Tenant A requests the object, download, signed URL, or evidence traversal.

scenario_results.given: Audit events, policy decisions, operator metrics, exports, and status records exist for multiple tenants.
scenario_results.implementation_area: RLS/policy for audit and policy tables, tenant-aware metrics/export layer.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Scoped exports, role matrix, denial logs, no cross-tenant event IDs.
scenario_results.then: Only authorized tenant records and aggregated non-sensitive metrics are visible; system-wide access requires explicit privileged policy.
scenario_results.verification_method: API/DB tests for tenant user, tenant admin, and system operator fixtures.
scenario_results.when: A tenant-scoped user queries or exports them.

scenario_results.given: The application connects to Postgres using its normal runtime role.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Dedicated migration owner and application roles, FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY, grants.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Role inventory showing rolsuper=false and rolbypassrls=false; relrowsecurity/relforcerowsecurity report.
scenario_results.then: The role is not superuser, table owner, or BYPASSRLS; protected tables have RLS enabled and forced where the design requires owner protection.
scenario_results.verification_method: Database metadata assertions over pg_roles, pg_class, pg_policies, and ownership.
scenario_results.when: Role and table security attributes are inspected and protected queries run.

scenario_results.given: An operator needs cross-tenant maintenance, recovery, or investigation.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Separate maintenance role/workflow, approval record, scoped admin commands.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Approval record, role assumption/revocation timestamps, accessed row counts, audit trail.
scenario_results.then: Normal app credentials cannot perform it; the privileged path requires explicit authorization, purpose, time bound, audit, and safe output handling.
scenario_results.verification_method: Integration rehearsal with denied app role and approved synthetic break-glass role.
scenario_results.when: A break-glass or maintenance path is requested.

scenario_results.given: A pooled database connection previously served tenant A.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Connection checkout/checkin hooks, SET LOCAL context, transaction discipline.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Connection IDs with tenant sequence, zero canary leakage, reset assertions.
scenario_results.then: Tenant context is transaction-local and reset; tenant B never sees tenant A state or rows.
scenario_results.verification_method: Pool stress test that alternates tenants and injects failures/cancellations.
scenario_results.when: The same physical connection is reused for tenant B, on success, error, timeout, cancellation, and rollback paths.

scenario_results.given: Concurrent tenant-A and tenant-B requests use the same service and pool.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Async context propagation, pool discipline, tenant-scoped caches.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Load transcript, per-request trace IDs, zero foreign canaries, no mixed audit refs.
scenario_results.then: Each request remains isolated; no cross-request context, cache, result, or audit correlation is mixed.
scenario_results.verification_method: Concurrent integration test with unique canaries and randomized scheduling.
scenario_results.when: Reads and writes interleave under load.

scenario_results.given: A background job, retry, scheduled task, extraction worker, or workflow continuation processes tenant-owned work.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Signed/validated job envelope, worker RequestContext, quarantine and retry policy.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Job envelope digest, worker decision, quarantine/retry record, scoped DB/audit trace.
scenario_results.then: Valid jobs run only in their tenant; missing/tampered jobs fail closed or quarantine; retries preserve scope and idempotency.
scenario_results.verification_method: Worker integration tests for valid, missing, changed, replayed, and cross-tenant envelopes.
scenario_results.when: The job executes with valid, missing, stale, or tampered scope metadata.

scenario_results.given: Protected data is accessed through SQL views, functions, stored procedures, ORM raw SQL, or security-definer code.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Database function/view security review, restricted EXECUTE grants, repository lint.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Object inventory, grants, cross-tenant tests, lint report.
scenario_results.then: No path bypasses tenant isolation; unsafe security-definer or owner-context access is rejected by CI or explicitly governed.
scenario_results.verification_method: Database integration tests plus static inventory of views/functions/security-definer objects.
scenario_results.when: The application role invokes each supported path.

scenario_results.given: Tenant A attempts writes that collide with tenant-B unique keys or reference tenant-B foreign keys.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Composite tenant keys, tenant-aware foreign keys, neutral error translation.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Schema definitions, neutral errors, unchanged data, no foreign identifiers.
scenario_results.then: Errors do not reveal tenant-B values/existence; keys are tenant-scoped where required, and unsafe cross-tenant references are impossible.
scenario_results.verification_method: DB/API tests for duplicate and foreign-key boundary cases.
scenario_results.when: The database validates constraints.

scenario_results.given: Existing local/scaffold records or imported archive rows are migrated to the VS2 schema.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Backfill mapping, quarantine table/report, NOT NULL/foreign-key constraints.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Migration report with counts/checksums, quarantine reasons, rollback transcript.
scenario_results.then: Known rows migrate deterministically; unknown/ambiguous rows are quarantined or block migration; no ownerless global truth is created.
scenario_results.verification_method: Migration tests on clean, mixed, malformed, duplicate, and rollback fixtures.
scenario_results.when: A row has known, missing, ambiguous, or invalid tenant/namespace ownership.

scenario_results.given: A migration adds or changes a tenant-bearing table, partition, materialized view, or policy.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Schema linter/inventory script, migration checklist, CI gate.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Schema inventory report, expected failing gate, passing migration evidence.
scenario_results.then: The gate fails unless tenant columns, RLS enable/force state, command policies, grants, and test coverage are declared.
scenario_results.verification_method: Failing fixture migration, passing corrected migration, machine-readable inventory.
scenario_results.when: CI runs the schema security inventory.

scenario_results.given: Two namespaces/workspaces exist inside the same tenant, such as Personal and Organization.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Namespace policy input, service authorization, scoped queries.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Allow/deny decisions, zero implicit context use, provenance on allowed promotion.
scenario_results.then: RLS tenant access alone is insufficient; service/OPA policy enforces namespace, owner, membership, classification, and explicit promotion rules.
scenario_results.verification_method: Integration matrix for same-tenant personal/org/team/project access.
scenario_results.when: A principal accesses, searches, or uses context across them.

scenario_results.given: A user attempts copy, reference, share, or promotion from tenant A to tenant B.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Cross-tenant invariant in domain service, OPA policy, RLS constraints.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Denied policy decision, zero target records, audit event, negative-evidence counters.
scenario_results.then: The operation is denied at product, policy, and storage layers; no target row, link, blob, or metadata is created.
scenario_results.verification_method: API/CLI/DB test for every transfer mode.
scenario_results.when: The transfer request is evaluated.

scenario_results.given: Caches, idempotency keys, deduplication indexes, locks, rate limits, and saved cursors are used by multiple tenants.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Tenant-prefixed cache/idempotency keys, composite indexes, scoped locks.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Key snapshots/digests, independent results, zero cross-tenant cache hits.
scenario_results.then: Keys are tenant-scoped unless intentionally global and non-sensitive; one tenant cannot read, suppress, replay, or infer another tenant's operation.
scenario_results.verification_method: Collision and replay tests across two tenants.
scenario_results.when: Two tenants submit identical IDs/content/keys or concurrent operations.

scenario_results.given: Tenant-scoped export and privileged full backup/restore paths exist.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Export service, backup role/procedure, restore verification, policy migration.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Backup manifest, restored counts/hashes, RLS inventory before/after, tenant export diff.
scenario_results.then: Tenant export contains only authorized rows; privileged backup is complete, restore preserves RLS/policies/audit, and filtered backups cannot silently omit protected rows.
scenario_results.verification_method: Automated local backup/restore and per-tenant export tests with row/hash counts.
scenario_results.when: Exports and a local backup→restore rehearsal run.

scenario_results.given: A protected data, tool, action, connector, model, policy-admin, or memory operation is requested.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Versioned PolicyInput schema, builders per enforcement point, schema validation.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Schema file, valid/invalid fixtures, input digest and source-of-attribute map.
scenario_results.then: Input follows one versioned schema containing trusted subject, tenant/namespace/workspace, resource, action, classification, mission authority, risk, data scope, connector/tool capability, and environment fields.
scenario_results.verification_method: Contract tests and golden inputs for every operation family.
scenario_results.when: The product constructs an OPA input document.

scenario_results.given: A principal has the required role and membership for a low-risk operation.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Rego RBAC rules and role data.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: opa test JSON, allow decision with bundle revision, downstream success/audit.
scenario_results.then: The request is allowed only for the declared resource/action/scope and the allow decision is auditable.
scenario_results.verification_method: OPA unit tests plus product integration allow cases.
scenario_results.when: OPA evaluates the operation.

scenario_results.given: A principal lacks a required role or approval authority.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Rego deny rules, reason mapping, enforcement adapter.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Denied decisions, zero side effects, stable error and audit references.
scenario_results.then: OPA denies with a non-leaking reason and safe resolution path; downstream mutation/egress does not occur.
scenario_results.verification_method: Role/action access-control matrix tests.
scenario_results.when: The principal reads, writes, approves, executes, or configures a protected resource.

scenario_results.given: Role alone would allow an operation, but attributes such as namespace, classification, mission authority, workspace mode, environment, or risk disallow it.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Rego ABAC rules, classification/risk taxonomy, mission/workspace inputs.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Allow/deny/escalate matrix, reason codes, policy coverage report.
scenario_results.then: ABAC conditions deny or escalate it; a matching allowed attribute set succeeds.
scenario_results.verification_method: Data-driven OPA tests at each attribute boundary.
scenario_results.when: OPA evaluates the request.

scenario_results.given: A resource type, action, tool, connector capability, model, or policy path is unknown or returns undefined.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Default-deny Rego rules and undefined-result adapter.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Denied outputs, no downstream calls, audit decision.
scenario_results.then: The normalized result is deny, never implicit allow, with an unknown-policy reason and resolution.
scenario_results.verification_method: OPA/product tests for typos, new enum values, missing rule paths, and undefined output.
scenario_results.when: The enforcement adapter evaluates it.

scenario_results.given: OPA input is partial, malformed, over-specified, wrong-versioned, or contains unexpected authoritative attributes.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Strict input schema, version negotiation, unknown-field policy, redaction.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: 400/deny transcripts, zero evaluation/side effects as appropriate, sanitized logs.
scenario_results.then: Schema validation fails closed; errors identify safe corrective action without echoing secrets or protected values.
scenario_results.verification_method: Fuzz/property tests and boundary fixtures.
scenario_results.when: The policy endpoint receives it.

scenario_results.given: The OPA service is running in the local/on-prem profile.
scenario_results.implementation_area: OPA hardened configuration, network binding, client authentication/authorization.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: OPA config, listening sockets, successful authorized call, denied unauthorized call.
scenario_results.then: OPA is bound to the intended interface, protected by the chosen authentication/transport controls, and management/data APIs are not anonymously exposed.
scenario_results.verification_method: Container/network tests from allowed and disallowed peers.
scenario_results.when: A local authorized client and an unauthorized/remote client access its APIs.

scenario_results.given: A request reaches the API gateway.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Gateway middleware/enforcement point and trace propagation.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Gateway decision, zero downstream counters, trace/audit record.
scenario_results.then: The request does not reach the service handler, database, tool runtime, model router, or connector.
scenario_results.verification_method: Integration test with handler/DB/tool call counters.
scenario_results.when: Gateway policy denies it.

scenario_results.given: A caller bypasses or originates behind the gateway and invokes a protected service method.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Service authorization decorator/interceptor and repository guards.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Service decision, zero repository/tool/connector calls, audit event.
scenario_results.then: No protected read, mutation, tool, model, or connector operation occurs.
scenario_results.verification_method: Direct service/API-internal integration tests.
scenario_results.when: Service-layer policy denies it.

scenario_results.given: A tool or Agent Pack receives a request that gateway/service policy allowed but runtime capability policy denies.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Capability sandbox, tool policy adapter, runtime audit.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Runtime denial, zero operations/bytes/calls, policy and audit refs.
scenario_results.then: The runtime blocks the undeclared capability and records negative evidence; upstream allow cannot bypass runtime enforcement.
scenario_results.verification_method: Real sandbox integration test for each capability class.
scenario_results.when: The tool attempts filesystem, environment, host, shell, or network access.

scenario_results.given: Service policy mistakenly allows or is bypassed for a cross-tenant database query.
scenario_results.implementation_area: RLS independent of service decision, anomaly logging.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Service allow + DB zero rows/denial, anomaly audit/metric.
scenario_results.then: Postgres RLS still prevents access, proving defense in depth; the anomaly is observable.
scenario_results.verification_method: Fault-injection integration test that stubs an allow at service layer.
scenario_results.when: The application role executes the query.

scenario_results.given: An ActionCard or WorkflowRun requests internal mutation, risky action, or external side effect.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Action policy entrypoints, risk rules, workflow gate.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Dry-run, policy decisions, approval state, execution/denial, audit timeline.
scenario_results.then: Allowed low-risk behavior follows mission/workspace authority; high-risk behavior escalates/requires approval; execution rechecks policy and cannot bypass Workflow/Action.
scenario_results.verification_method: Action lifecycle integration matrix.
scenario_results.when: Policy evaluates dry-run, approval, and execution phases.

scenario_results.given: A workspace policy allows or disallows model providers based on classification or on-prem requirements.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Policy-aware model router, provider capability metadata.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Routing decision, zero disallowed provider calls, explanation and audit refs.
scenario_results.then: Disallowed providers are blocked or safely rerouted with explanation; no request/data is sent to them.
scenario_results.verification_method: Router integration test with local_test and denied external provider fixtures.
scenario_results.when: The model router selects a provider.

scenario_results.given: A workflow, agent, or extension requests provider data, credentials, raw access, or an external action.
scenario_results.implementation_area: ConnectorHub handoff envelope, capability policy, credential references only.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Setup/preflight result, policy decision, zero raw secrets, connector and CornerStone audit refs.
scenario_results.then: Only declared ConnectorHub-mediated capabilities proceed; raw credentials/provider clients/direct API handles remain inaccessible; CornerStone retains product approval ownership.
scenario_results.verification_method: Contract tests with declared/undeclared capability and raw-secret probes.
scenario_results.when: Policy and ConnectorHub capability checks run.

scenario_results.given: The Rego policy bundle contains all VS2 entrypoints.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Rego unit/data-driven tests, entrypoint manifest, CI commands.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: OPA test JSON, coverage JSON, entrypoint coverage map, exit code 0.
scenario_results.then: All positive, negative, malformed, boundary, and regression cases pass; no-test execution fails; a machine-readable coverage report is produced and reviewed against declared entrypoints.
scenario_results.verification_method: Run `opa test ... --fail-on-empty --coverage --format=json` plus bundle build/check.
scenario_results.when: CI runs policy tests.

scenario_results.given: A valid new policy bundle is published.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Versioned bundle build/service, revision metadata, status monitoring.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: OPA status before/after, revision transition, decision sample set, zero mixed/undefined results.
scenario_results.then: Activation is atomic; decisions identify the active revision; no request observes a partial mix; the previous revision remains traceable.
scenario_results.verification_method: Concurrent evaluation during bundle update.
scenario_results.when: OPA downloads and activates it while requests continue.

scenario_results.given: A bundle is malformed, fails Rego tests/compile, has incompatible schema, or cannot activate.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Pre-activation validation, persisted last-known-good, readiness/status integration.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: OPA status/error, unchanged active revision, decisions under failure, operator alert.
scenario_results.then: Activation fails, the last known good bundle remains active (or protected operations fail closed if none exists), and status/error is visible without exposing secrets.
scenario_results.verification_method: Bundle failure injection tests including first-start failure.
scenario_results.when: The update is attempted.

scenario_results.given: OPA is unavailable, slow, returns invalid output, or times out.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Policy client timeout/circuit breaker, fail-closed adapter, readiness.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Stable denial/error, zero side effects, readiness/metric/audit evidence.
scenario_results.then: The operation fails closed or follows an explicitly documented safe read-only fallback; readiness degrades, and no risky side effect/egress occurs.
scenario_results.verification_method: Fault-injection tests for connection refused, timeout, 5xx, malformed JSON.
scenario_results.when: A protected read, write, tool, action, connector, or model request is made.

scenario_results.given: An allow decision is cached and policy/membership is then revoked or bundle revision changes.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Revision-aware decision cache and invalidation.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Cache keys/digests, new decision revision, denial, zero stale allows.
scenario_results.then: The stale allow is not reused beyond the documented bound; cache keys include tenant, principal, resource, action, and revision.
scenario_results.verification_method: Allow→revoke/update→retry tests across tenants.
scenario_results.when: The same operation is retried.

scenario_results.given: Policy decisions contain potentially sensitive input fields.
scenario_results.implementation_area: OPA decision logging/masking policy, audit adapter, redaction.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Decision log with masked fields, CornerStone audit ref, zero secret scanner findings.
scenario_results.then: Each event has decision_id, policy path/revision, tenant-safe correlation, and masked/redacted sensitive fields; logs are linkable but not a secret store.
scenario_results.verification_method: Secret-bearing fixture through allow and deny decisions.
scenario_results.when: Decision logging and CornerStone audit mirroring run.

scenario_results.given: A user proposes a policy, role, tenant-isolation, egress, retention, or audit configuration change.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Policy-admin ActionCard/Workflow, bundle pipeline, rollback metadata.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Action/workflow records, bundle revisions, approvals, before/after decisions, rollback transcript.
scenario_results.then: It is represented as a sensitive governed change with diff, impact, tests, policy decision, authorized approval, version, rollback, and audit; direct mutation is blocked.
scenario_results.verification_method: Integration test for draft→dry-run→approve→activate→rollback and direct bypass denial.
scenario_results.when: The change is reviewed/applied.

scenario_results.given: A user is denied data, action, model, connector, tool, egress, or policy-admin access.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Reason-code catalog, error translation, UI denial component.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Response snapshots/screenshots, stable codes, matching decision/audit refs, leak scan.
scenario_results.then: The response states safe cause, impact, retry/request-access/change-scope/approver resolution where applicable, and what remains preserved, without exposing protected data.
scenario_results.verification_method: Golden API/CLI/browser tests for major denial classes.
scenario_results.when: The error is shown through API, CLI, and UI.

scenario_results.given: Policy input/output is at, below, and above configured size/depth/list limits, or contains unknown enum values.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Documented policy limits, parser/schema guards, time/memory limits.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Limit configuration, test corpus, resource metrics, stable failures.
scenario_results.then: At/below-limit requests behave deterministically; over-limit/unsupported requests fail safely with helpful errors, bounded resource use, and no partial side effect.
scenario_results.verification_method: Boundary-value and property/fuzz tests using configuration-derived N-1/N/N+1 cases.
scenario_results.when: The request is evaluated.

scenario_results.given: Multiple policy rules apply, including an allow and a restrictive deny/escalation.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Decision aggregation policy, explicit deny/escalate precedence.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Conflict matrix, final reason list, bundle revision, no accidental allow.
scenario_results.then: The documented conservative precedence is deterministic; restrictive tenant/security/risk rules cannot be accidentally overridden by a permissive rule.
scenario_results.verification_method: OPA data-driven conflict tests.
scenario_results.when: The aggregate decision is computed.

scenario_results.given: The same policy input is evaluated at gateway, service, tool runtime, and native CLI.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Shared schema/client, revision pinning/correlation, mismatch guard.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Decision comparison report, mismatch denial, anomaly audit/metric.
scenario_results.then: Materially equivalent decisions are produced; any mismatch fails closed, emits an anomaly, and cannot lead to a side effect.
scenario_results.verification_method: Cross-enforcement conformance test and deliberate mismatch injection.
scenario_results.when: All enforcement points evaluate it against the same active revision.

scenario_results.given: A real tool/runtime process has no egress grant and a local egress-sink endpoint is reachable at the network layer.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Sandbox/network policy or enforced egress proxy, denial adapter.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Denied tool result, sink access log with zero requests, network counter, policy/audit refs.
scenario_results.then: The connection is blocked by the runtime/network boundary, not merely skipped by application logic; the sink records zero requests/bytes.
scenario_results.verification_method: Container/process integration test with local egress sink and packet/request counters.
scenario_results.when: The process attempts an outbound connection using supported HTTP and socket paths.

scenario_results.given: A tool/workflow has a declared ConnectorHub capability, tenant/workspace authorization, allowed mock destination, and required approval.
scenario_results.implementation_area: ConnectorHub-mediated egress adapter, allow policy, mock provider.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: One sink request, sanitized request metadata, Action/Workflow/Connector result, decision/audit refs.
scenario_results.then: Exactly the declared call succeeds; evidence, policy, approval, connector result, and audit are linked; no undeclared call occurs.
scenario_results.verification_method: End-to-end local integration test.
scenario_results.when: It calls the local mock provider through the governed egress path.

scenario_results.given: Tenant A has an egress allow rule and tenant B does not.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Tenant-scoped egress policy data and cache keys.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: A success/B denial, separate decisions, sink count exactly one.
scenario_results.then: Tenant A follows its governed path; tenant B is denied; allowlist/cache/config does not bleed across tenants.
scenario_results.verification_method: Two-tenant egress integration test.
scenario_results.when: Both tenants request the same destination/capability.

scenario_results.given: An egress policy declares allowed scheme, hostname, port, method, path/capability, and data scope.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Canonical URL/destination parser, structured allow rules.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Per-case decisions, sink logs, normalization output, zero unexpected calls.
scenario_results.then: Only exact normalized permitted combinations pass; case, trailing-dot, encoded path, alternate port, wildcard, or method variations cannot broaden access.
scenario_results.verification_method: Table-driven boundary tests.
scenario_results.when: Calls vary each field and use URL normalization variants.

scenario_results.given: A caller targets IP literals, loopback, private, link-local, multicast, Unix sockets, cloud metadata ranges, or a public name resolving to them.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Resolved-address validation, reserved-range policy, internal-capability model.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Denied decisions, zero sink/metadata requests, sanitized destination class.
scenario_results.then: The runtime denies the destination before sending data and records a safe reason.
scenario_results.verification_method: IPv4/IPv6 destination matrix against local fixtures.
scenario_results.when: The tool attempts connection without a purpose-specific internal capability.

scenario_results.given: An allowed hostname resolves first to an allowed address and then to a denied/private address, or resolves to mixed addresses.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Resolver integration, address pinning/revalidation, connection guard.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: DNS transcript, selected/blocked addresses, zero denied-address connections.
scenario_results.then: Resolved addresses are validated at connection time; denied addresses are never contacted; policy cannot be bypassed by DNS rebinding.
scenario_results.verification_method: Deterministic fake-DNS rebinding test.
scenario_results.when: Connection is attempted across resolution/retry.

scenario_results.given: An allowed endpoint redirects to a denied host, scheme, port, or path.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Redirect-aware governed HTTP client.
scenario_results.notes: Existing egress checks prove zero attempted external calls, not an enforced runtime/network boundary with controlled sink proof.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Hop-by-hop decisions, sink logs, header redaction, bounded redirect count.
scenario_results.then: Every hop is re-authorized; the denied hop is blocked, redirect limits apply, and no sensitive header is forwarded across unauthorized boundaries.
scenario_results.verification_method: Mock redirect-chain tests including loops and cross-host redirects.
scenario_results.when: The HTTP client follows redirects.

scenario_results.given: A tool attempts to bypass governed HTTP using proxy environment variables, direct sockets, alternate DNS, WebSocket/FTP/SMTP, subprocess, or a bundled client.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Network namespace/proxy enforcement, environment sanitization, syscall/capability restrictions.
scenario_results.notes: Existing egress checks prove zero attempted external calls, not an enforced runtime/network boundary with controlled sink proof.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Zero unauthorized connections/processes, denied capability records, runtime audit.
scenario_results.then: Undeclared protocols and paths remain blocked at the network/capability boundary; no arbitrary shell/host privilege is gained.
scenario_results.verification_method: Adversarial sandbox integration suite.
scenario_results.when: The process runs in the VS2 sandbox.

scenario_results.given: An Artifact, web page, connector payload, tool output, or prompt contains instructions to call a URL, exfiltrate data, change policy, or approve an action.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Untrusted-content labeling, agent/tool boundary, policy gate.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Zero tool/action/egress calls, blocked-attempt audit, untrusted label/evidence refs.
scenario_results.then: It remains untrusted evidence; it cannot create authority, egress grants, policy changes, approvals, or tool/action execution.
scenario_results.verification_method: Prompt-injection red-team fixtures across artifact and connector paths.
scenario_results.when: The content is processed by an agent/workflow.

scenario_results.given: An allowed external call needs provider authentication or contains sensitive payload fields.
scenario_results.implementation_area: ConnectorHub credential custody, request projection/redaction, log masking.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Sink metadata without raw secret, zero secret scanner findings, credential reference only.
scenario_results.then: Agents/product outputs never receive raw credentials; least data is sent; denied calls send nothing; logs/decisions/audit mask secrets and sensitive payload.
scenario_results.verification_method: Secret-canary integration tests for allowed and denied calls.
scenario_results.when: The governed ConnectorHub path prepares and executes it.

scenario_results.given: An Action dry-run predicts external calls and is then approved or delayed.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Dry-run fingerprint, policy revision binding, execution recheck.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Dry-run and execution decision revisions, stale-state denial, zero sink calls.
scenario_results.then: Execution detects stale dry-run state, re-evaluates current policy and impact, and blocks or requires renewed approval; it never relies solely on the old allow.
scenario_results.verification_method: Allow-at-dry-run → revoke/change → execute test.
scenario_results.when: Policy, allowlist, data, approval, or destination changes before execution.

scenario_results.given: An allowed external operation times out, returns retryable failure, or receives duplicate execution requests.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Workflow retry/timeout/idempotency, ConnectorHub action keys.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Attempt timeline, one side effect maximum, idempotency key scope, compensation/audit record.
scenario_results.then: Timeout/retry limits are bounded, idempotency is tenant-scoped, and duplicate real/mock side effects are prevented or explicitly compensated.
scenario_results.verification_method: Mock provider fault and duplicate-request tests.
scenario_results.when: Workflow retry logic runs.

scenario_results.given: Allowed and denied egress attempts occur.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Egress audit schema, metrics, trace correlation, redaction.
scenario_results.notes: Existing egress checks prove zero attempted external calls, not an enforced runtime/network boundary with controlled sink proof.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Correlated records, counter assertions, decision IDs, secret-scan report.
scenario_results.then: Records correlate tenant-safe trace, decision, action/workflow, connector capability, normalized destination class, outcome, and byte/call counts without storing secrets or raw payloads.
scenario_results.verification_method: Integration plus log/metric contract tests.
scenario_results.when: Operators inspect policy, connector, runtime, and CornerStone audit/metrics.

scenario_results.given: The egress controller/proxy/sandbox policy component is unavailable or misconfigured.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Fail-closed sandbox wiring, readiness dependencies, no direct-client fallback.
scenario_results.notes: Existing egress checks prove zero attempted external calls, not an enforced runtime/network boundary with controlled sink proof.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Degraded readiness, denied operations, zero sink/host counters, operator guidance.
scenario_results.then: Protected capabilities fail closed; readiness reports the degraded component; no fallback direct connection or host access occurs.
scenario_results.verification_method: Stop/disable controller and repeat capability tests.
scenario_results.when: A tool attempts network, shell, filesystem, environment, or host access.

scenario_results.given: A major VS2 allow, deny, missing-context, policy-unavailable, RLS, and egress failure is exercised.
scenario_results.implementation_area: CLI/API/UI response contracts and error catalog.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: CLI/API payloads, screenshots/DOM snapshot, schema validation, matching references.
scenario_results.then: Machine-readable schemas, exit/status codes, user messages, evidence_refs, policy_decision_refs, and audit_refs remain semantically consistent.
scenario_results.verification_method: Golden contract tests and browser proof.
scenario_results.when: The behavior is observed through CLI JSON/text, HTTP API, and UI.

scenario_results.given: VS2 generates access, denial, policy update, RLS anomaly, egress, approval, connector, and rollback events.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Audit event contract, hash-chain/checkpoint integration, verifier.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Event inventory, verification success, tamper detection failure evidence.
scenario_results.then: Required events are append-only/tamper-evident, queryable by tenant/action/decision, and modification/removal is detected.
scenario_results.verification_method: Audit contract tests and tamper fixture.
scenario_results.when: Audit verification and deliberate tampering run.

scenario_results.given: An operator monitors Postgres, OPA, egress control, policy denials, RLS anomalies, retries, and audit integrity.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Health/readiness, metrics, trace IDs, operator status surface.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Status JSON/dashboard snapshot, metrics samples, alert events, leak scan.
scenario_results.then: Health/readiness distinguishes component states, metrics are actionable and tenant-safe, and alerts can identify failure without exposing protected data.
scenario_results.verification_method: Component fault-injection and observability contract tests.
scenario_results.when: Status, metrics, logs, and traces are queried.

scenario_results.given: VS2 database/policy/runtime changes are deployed over the existing local scaffold and VS1 data.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Postgres store adapter, migration versions, compatibility layer, rollback scripts.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Before/after object counts/hashes, regression reports, rollback transcript, no data loss.
scenario_results.then: The smallest compatible adapter path preserves existing artifacts/evidence/claims/ontology/audit; rollback is deterministic; no destructive migration runs without approval.
scenario_results.verification_method: Fresh and upgrade-path integration tests with checksums and existing VS0/VS1 fixtures.
scenario_results.when: Forward migration, compatibility run, failed migration, and rollback are exercised.

scenario_results.given: A new evaluator uses the documented local/on-prem VS2 quickstart without live provider credentials.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Compose/local profile, migrations, seed policy, health/readiness, quickstart verifier.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Command transcript with exit codes, component versions, policy revision, RLS inventory, generated IDs, elapsed time.
scenario_results.then: Readiness succeeds only when required components and active policy/RLS inventory are valid; a tenant-isolation and egress-deny smoke flow completes.
scenario_results.verification_method: Executable clean-environment quickstart.
scenario_results.when: They start CornerStone, Postgres, OPA, and the local egress test harness.

scenario_results.given: A feature/milestone report claims VS2 completion.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Native `cornerstone scenario verify vs2-policy-tenancy-egress`, gate, Make target, release evidence package.
scenario_results.notes: The native VS2 verifier was absent at baseline; this current-state verifier now records that the AI-verifiable implementation evidence is still missing.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Machine-readable report, command transcript, evidence manifest/hashes, base/final revision metadata, verdict.
scenario_results.then: All applicable MUST_PASS and REGRESSION rows have concrete evidence; human rows are explicit; report metadata binds to verified revision/tree; no production/live overclaim is present.
scenario_results.verification_method: Scenario report schema validation, gate command, report review.
scenario_results.when: The native scenario verifier and gate run against the exact code/tree state.

scenario_results.given: VS2 changes are applied.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Compatibility adapters and regression suite.
scenario_results.notes: Existing egress checks prove zero attempted external calls, not an enforced runtime/network boundary with controlled sink proof.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Existing scenario reports with exit code 0 and no weakened assertions.
scenario_results.then: The VS0 local product loop remains green and evidence/action safety is unchanged.
scenario_results.verification_method: Run existing VS0 EVUX/runtime/operator gates.
scenario_results.when: The existing VS0 Artifact→Search→Evidence→Claim→Action→Audit gates run.

scenario_results.given: VS2 changes are applied over the accepted local VS1 ontology slice.
scenario_results.implementation_area: VS1 data adapter and policy-aware ontology services.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: VS1 report, zero auto-promotions/cross-tenant promotions, policy refs.
scenario_results.then: Ontology suggestions remain draft until explicit promotion; search/profile/claim/action/audit integration remains green under tenant policy.
scenario_results.verification_method: Run `make verify-vs1-ontology` plus cross-tenant ontology tests.
scenario_results.when: The VS1 suggest/review/promote suite runs.

scenario_results.given: Personal context exists in a user's personal namespace.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Namespace policy and retrieval guards.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Zero personal refs/canary, denial or insufficient-evidence response.
scenario_results.then: Personal context is not used, cited, searched, or exposed.
scenario_results.verification_method: Known-canary personal→organization leak test.
scenario_results.when: An organization request is answered or acted on without explicit promotion/permission.

scenario_results.given: Organization context exists and the user opens a personal workspace.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Reverse namespace guards.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Zero organization refs/canary, policy/audit outcome.
scenario_results.then: Organization context is not used or exposed.
scenario_results.verification_method: Organization→personal canary leak test.
scenario_results.when: A related personal query/action runs without an allowed reference.

scenario_results.given: Product-learning or benchmark processes run.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Product-learning namespace policy.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Denied decision, zero source rows and memory writes.
scenario_results.then: Access is denied and no hidden memory/truth rewrite occurs.
scenario_results.verification_method: Policy/data-access test.
scenario_results.when: They request raw personal/organization truth without opt-in/approved redaction.

scenario_results.given: A prompt/document claims the agent now has admin, tenant, connector, tool, or egress authority.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Prompt-injection and authority guards.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Zero authority changes/tool calls/egress, blocked-attempt audit.
scenario_results.then: Authority remains defined by trusted role, workspace, mission, connector capability, and policy state.
scenario_results.verification_method: Red-team fixture suite.
scenario_results.when: The agent processes it.

scenario_results.given: An Agent Pack or product handler tries to use provider credentials or direct provider networking.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Pack validation, import/runtime restrictions, egress policy.
scenario_results.notes: Current policy behavior is a deterministic local scaffold; no OPA/Rego process, bundle lifecycle, decision log, or fail-closed adapter evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Validation failure/quarantine, zero credential/network exposure.
scenario_results.then: The pack/path is blocked or quarantined; ConnectorHub remains the provider boundary.
scenario_results.verification_method: Static and runtime direct-provider tests.
scenario_results.when: Validation/runtime executes.

scenario_results.given: A developer adds a direct repository/database/writeback path that skips Workflow/Action or service policy.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Boundary lint, repository interfaces, RLS.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Gate failure or runtime denial, unchanged state, audit/anomaly evidence.
scenario_results.then: The bypass is rejected by architecture lint, service guard, RLS, or runtime enforcement and cannot mutate state.
scenario_results.verification_method: Deliberate bypass fixture/test.
scenario_results.when: CI and integration tests run.

scenario_results.given: A new tool, model, workflow, policy entrypoint, connector capability, or table is introduced.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Audit event manifest and coverage gate.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Failing omission proof, passing corrected event inventory.
scenario_results.then: All required critical events and references remain present; coverage cannot silently drop.
scenario_results.verification_method: Mutation test that omits an event.
scenario_results.when: Audit contract tests run.

scenario_results.given: Configuration is missing, reset, partially upgraded, or a new workspace/tenant is created.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Secure defaults and bootstrap validation.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Default config snapshot, denial suite, RLS/role inventory.
scenario_results.then: Egress is denied, policy is default-deny, app role cannot bypass RLS, high-risk actions require approval, and shell/host access remains blocked.
scenario_results.verification_method: Fresh/reset/partial-config integration tests.
scenario_results.when: Security defaults initialize.

scenario_results.given: Policy or membership is revoked after an allow was cached.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Cache and revision guard.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Decision revision sequence, zero post-revocation successes.
scenario_results.then: No stale allow produces access or side effect beyond the frozen revocation bound.
scenario_results.verification_method: High-concurrency revocation test.
scenario_results.when: Requests continue during cache/bundle transition.

scenario_results.given: Cross-tenant, malformed, secret-bearing, and denied requests generate errors/logs/traces/screenshots/reports.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Central redaction and safe error schemas.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Zero scanner findings or reviewed allowlist with justification.
scenario_results.then: No secret, protected payload, raw credential, or foreign-tenant identifier appears unnecessarily.
scenario_results.verification_method: Canary/secret scanner across generated outputs.
scenario_results.when: Leak scanners and snapshot tests run.

scenario_results.given: A new durable tenant-bearing table or partition is added without RLS/policy coverage.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Schema/RLS inventory gate.
scenario_results.notes: No Postgres/RLS SQL, local DB profile, migration, hardened app role, or two-tenant integration evidence exists yet.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Expected failing gate and remediation evidence.
scenario_results.then: The build fails before merge/release.
scenario_results.verification_method: Negative migration fixture.
scenario_results.when: Schema CI runs.

scenario_results.given: Tenant tests run in parallel or retry after failures.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Unique test tenant IDs, cleanup, deterministic concurrency harness.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Repeatable report hashes/semantics, zero cross-fixture canaries.
scenario_results.then: Test state remains isolated; one test/tenant cannot contaminate another or create false PASS.
scenario_results.verification_method: Parallel/repeat test execution.
scenario_results.when: Test fixtures, pools, caches, queues, and reports share infrastructure.

scenario_results.given: Policy, tenant, and connector controls are added.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Product navigation and progressive-disclosure UX.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: UI map/screenshots, absence of required repo-name workflow.
scenario_results.then: Users still experience one CornerStone product; repo/engine names are not required mental models, while admin security detail remains available.
scenario_results.verification_method: Automated nav/DOM check plus human review in H06.
scenario_results.when: A normal user navigates the product.

scenario_results.given: Reports, README, UI, or release metadata describe VS2.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Claim vocabulary guard and release-report validator.
scenario_results.notes: VS2-specific enforcement and evidence are not implemented in the current local scaffold.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Zero overclaim findings; explicit scope/human-required table.
scenario_results.then: Local/integration proof is not described as production, live-provider, penetration-tested, or human-accepted without separate evidence.
scenario_results.verification_method: Static/report lint and evidence manifest review.
scenario_results.when: A reviewer compares claims with evidence.

scenario_results.given: VS2 proposes an actual OPA production dependency, Postgres/RLS tenant model, authz semantics, and migration of durable state.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Architecture/ADR and approval workflow.
scenario_results.notes: Requires owner, security, network, IdP, live-provider, migration, or subjective UX evidence outside AI automation.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Dated APPROVE/REJECT record with approved scope, exceptions, rollback owner, and dependency decision.
scenario_results.then: JiYong/Tars or authorized owner approves the architecture, dependency, migration scope, rollback plan, and security ownership before code changes that cross the gate.
scenario_results.verification_method: Human architecture/security review.
scenario_results.when: Implementation is ready to start.

scenario_results.given: A production-like deployment contains multiple real or representative tenants.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Production-like environment and security test plan.
scenario_results.notes: Requires owner, security, network, IdP, live-provider, migration, or subjective UX evidence outside AI automation.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Signed report, test scope/topology, findings, remediation, re-test evidence.
scenario_results.then: No cross-tenant data/metadata leak or policy bypass is found, or findings are remediated and re-tested.
scenario_results.verification_method: Independent penetration/security review.
scenario_results.when: An independent security reviewer performs tenant-isolation and policy-bypass testing.

scenario_results.given: A real OIDC/SSO or enterprise identity provider is selected.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Identity provider integration and mapping policy.
scenario_results.notes: Requires owner, security, network, IdP, live-provider, migration, or subjective UX evidence outside AI automation.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Redacted login/mapping/revocation transcript and owner/security approval.
scenario_results.then: Authorized stakeholders confirm mappings and revocation behavior; no synthetic-fixture claim is promoted to real IdP readiness.
scenario_results.verification_method: Approved real-IdP test.
scenario_results.when: Groups, roles, attributes, revocation, and tenant memberships are mapped.

scenario_results.given: The target production/on-prem network topology, DNS, proxy, firewall, service mesh, and sandbox are available.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Production network controls.
scenario_results.notes: Requires owner, security, network, IdP, live-provider, migration, or subjective UX evidence outside AI automation.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Topology diagram, firewall/proxy/network-policy evidence, packet/log transcript, approval.
scenario_results.then: All undeclared paths are blocked and approved paths are traceable under the real topology.
scenario_results.verification_method: Network control review and egress test.
scenario_results.when: A network/security operator verifies deny-by-default and approved exceptions outside the local harness.

scenario_results.given: Authorized live ConnectorHub credentials and provider permissions are available.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Live ConnectorHub integration.
scenario_results.notes: Requires owner, security, network, IdP, live-provider, migration, or subjective UX evidence outside AI automation.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Redacted provider transcript, approval, ConnectorHub/CornerStone audit refs, result/evidence refs.
scenario_results.then: Credential custody, declared capability, source policy, product approval, egress, result, evidence, and audit boundaries hold without secret exposure.
scenario_results.verification_method: Approved live-provider rehearsal.
scenario_results.when: A low-risk read-only or explicitly approved test action is executed.

scenario_results.given: A human operator uses tenant/workspace switching, denial guidance, policy admin, egress dry-run, approval, and audit surfaces.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Admin/operator UX.
scenario_results.notes: Requires owner, security, network, IdP, live-provider, migration, or subjective UX evidence outside AI automation.
scenario_results.required_evidence: ACCEPT/REJECT note, screenshots/recording, task outcomes, confusion/issues list.
scenario_results.then: They accept or reject whether boundaries, causes, risks, and recovery paths are understandable and not misleading.
scenario_results.verification_method: Human usability/trust review.
scenario_results.when: The operator completes representative tasks.

scenario_results.given: Real production-like data, backup tooling, retention rules, and rollback authority are available.
scenario_results.implementation_area: Production migration/backup/restore process.
scenario_results.notes: Requires owner, security, network, IdP, live-provider, migration, or subjective UX evidence outside AI automation.
scenario_results.required_evidence: Signed transcript, before/after counts/hashes, RLS/policy inventory, restore/rollback result.
scenario_results.then: Data/evidence/audit integrity, tenant ownership, RLS policies, and service recovery meet the approved plan.
scenario_results.verification_method: Human-supervised non-destructive rehearsal.
scenario_results.when: The approved VS2 migration and rollback/restore drill runs.
